plan: Install ESLint and required plugins: `npm install -D eslint @eslint/js typescript-eslint` (flat config ecosystem, no legacy plugins needed)

Create `eslint.config.js` using the flat config format with typescript-eslint: extend recommended JS rules + typescript-eslint recommended rules, set parser to typescript-eslint, target `src/**/*.ts` files, ignore `dist/` and `node_modules/`
Update the `lint` script in package.json from the placeholder echo to `"eslint src/"`
Verify `typecheck` script already works (`tsc --noEmit` is already configured in package.json with strict tsconfig.json) — run it to confirm no errors
Verify `test` script already works (`vitest run` is already configured) — run it to confirm the existing test suite passes

Create a sample unit test at `tests/us-002-tooling.test.ts` that validates: (a) ESLint config is loadable, (b) all three npm scripts exist in package.json, (c) a trivial assertion to prove Vitest runs
Run `npm run lint` and fix any issues ESLint reports on existing `src/` files
Run `npm run typecheck` end-to-end to confirm zero errors
Run `npm run test` end-to-end to confirm all tests pass including the new sample test
Verify all three scripts run non-interactively (no TTY prompts) by running them in sequence: `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test`
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run lint` on existing src/ files and confirm clean exit (exit code 0)
Run `npm run typecheck` and confirm zero TypeScript errors
Run `npm run test` and confirm all test suites pass, including the new sample test
Run all three commands in a single chain (`npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test`) to verify no interactive prompts block execution
Intentionally introduce a lint error (e.g., `any` type) and confirm ESLint catches it, then revert
Confirm `npx eslint --print-config src/main.ts` outputs valid config to verify ESLint config is correctly loaded
risks: ESLint flat config (`eslint.config.js`) requires ESLint v9+ — must ensure compatible version is installed
typescript-eslint recommended rules may flag issues in existing code (e.g., `us-001-workspace-init.test.ts` or `src/main.ts`) requiring minor fixes
Vitest already has no config file — default config works but may need a `vitest.config.ts` if test file discovery patterns need customization for the `tests/` directory
